body.title: [sprint] Identity resolvers: DB-URL / IGOR_NAME / hostname — call-time, raise-if-unset, NO module-scope binding
body.intention: Code reads identity through single resolvers that resolve at call time and raise a guided error if unset — never a baked credential, never a crash at import.
body.description:
**Problem:** ~91 files reinvent `os.environ.get('UU_HOME_DB_URL', '<hardcoded creds>')`. Provide the single chokepoints the sweeps will point at.

**Shape:** Consolidate/extend the existing resolvers (devices/igor/paths.py home_db_url already reads env+raises; unseen_university/db_proxy.py). Provide: a DB-URL resolver, an IGOR_NAME/instance resolver, and a hostname resolver (socket.gethostname()). ALL resolve at CALL time and raise a clear, guided error when the env is unset — NO baked fallback.

**CRITICAL (advisor):** the resolvers must NOT be called at module/import scope, and the sweeps that follow must convert any eager module-level binding (e.g. tree_index.py:37 `_DB_URL = _paths().home_db_url`) to lazy. Raise-if-unset at import scope would crash ~91 modules when the env is unset — breaking the rescueclaude recovery contract and worsening the test-collection errors. This ticket establishes the lazy pattern + a helper if useful.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/db_proxy.py, devices/igor/paths.py (HIGH-inertia — surface for approval), possibly a new unseen_university/identity.py.
**Design rules:** no-creds-in-source; lazy/call-time resolution; raise-with-guidance; log-state-changes.
**Scope boundary:** IN — the resolvers + the documented lazy pattern + their unit tests. OUT — editing the 91 call sites (the sweeps).
**Test plan:** resolver returns env value when set; raises a guided error (not KeyError) when unset; importing the resolver module with UU_HOME_DB_URL unset does NOT raise.
**Completion criteria:** unit test: resolver raises a guided message when unset, returns the value when set; `python -c 'import <resolver_module>'` succeeds with UU_HOME_DB_URL unset (no import-time resolution).

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/ is the log hierarchy. Never write to a flat — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle: `rollback()` must be idempotent — safe to call even if — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-001 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle)
[error] PATTERN-004: Flat-File Registry (Atomic Write): The skeleton must never depend on Postgres — skeleto — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-004 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-004: Flat-File Registry (Atomic Write))
…and 37 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
